evals.prompt: Review the attached feature file against the attached account sign-in and account-locking requirements. Return oracle used, oracle sufficiency, confidence, severity-ranked findings, a short traceability and coverage assessment, and the top recommendations.
evals.expected_output: A findings-first review that cites the authoritative source, lowers confidence only as needed, flags UI-driven language, and calls out any coverage or traceability gaps without inventing metadata.
expectations: Names the oracle sources used and reports oracle sufficiency explicitly.
Ranks material review findings by severity before recommendations.
Flags the UI-scripted sign-in scenario or similarly weak business readability.
Avoids inventing new requirement IDs, tags, or unsupported business rules.

evals.prompt: Rewrite the attached discount scenarios so they are stakeholder-facing, preserve the intended rules, split overloaded behavior when needed, and list unsupported assumptions separately.
evals.expected_output: A cleaner set of business-readable scenarios that preserve intent, improve naming and outcomes, and separate assumptions from confirmed behavior.
expectations: Preserves the Gold-customer discount intent instead of changing the business rule.
Improves scenario names and observable outcomes.
Splits or clearly repairs the overloaded checkout scenario.
Lists unsupported assumptions separately instead of fabricating details.

evals.prompt: Generate a BDD feature file from the attached user story and acceptance criteria. Include the supported main path and expired-link rejection path, and list unresolved assumptions separately.
evals.expected_output: A generated feature file for password reset that covers the supported main and rejection paths while keeping missing rules explicit.
expectations: Uses the provided user story and acceptance criteria as source inputs.
Generates a coherent feature with a valid-link success path.
Includes the expired-link rejection path because it is explicitly supported.
Calls out the unresolved second-reset-link rule as an assumption or gap.

evals.prompt: Review and improve the attached password reset draft against the attached acceptance criteria. Keep the findings short, then provide the corrected artifact and list open assumptions.
evals.expected_output: A hybrid response that makes oracle sufficiency and confidence explicit, highlights the most material defects, and follows with a corrected password-reset feature.
expectations: Uses hybrid mode with short findings before the rewritten artifact.
Keeps oracle sufficiency and confidence explicit.
Adds or repairs the expired-link rejection path because the acceptance criteria support it.
Keeps the unresolved second-link invalidation rule explicit instead of inventing behavior.

evals.prompt: Produce a formal BDD review report for the attached feature file against the attached requirements. Include an executive summary, oracle sufficiency and confidence, severity-ranked findings with evidence, traceability and coverage assessment, coaching recommendations, and an action plan.
evals.expected_output: A formal report that follows the review template, separates confirmed defects from missing information, and gives actionable coaching advice.
expectations: Uses a formal report structure rather than the default lightweight review contract.
Distinguishes findings from missing information or blocked decisions.
Includes traceability or coverage assessment tied to the supplied requirements.
Provides coaching recommendations and an action plan.
